theme: The Fairway Map — User Flow Tracking

shots.title: Flow types + validation functions
shots.notes: Created packages/core/src/flows.ts with FlowStep, FlowDefinition, FlowsFile types and parseFlows, validateFlows, checkFlowStaleness, loadFlows functions. Added flowsPath to SlopeConfig. 24 tests, all passed first run.

shots.title: slope flows CLI command
shots.notes: Created flows.ts CLI with init/list/check subcommands. Registered in dispatch. 8 tests, all passed first run.

shots.title: MCP search integration

hazards.description: Used require() in ESM module for dynamic config loading — had to refactor to direct JSON parse from filesystem

shots.notes: Added flows module to MCP search Zod enum, handleFlowsQuery with filtering, registry entries + SLOPE_TYPES definitions. 9 tests passed.

shots.title: CODEBASE.md flows section + stale-flows guard

hazards.description: Guard count test expected 11, needed updating to 12 after adding stale-flows guard

shots.notes: Added generateFlowsSummary to map.ts with auto-section markers, flows metadata count. Added stale-flows to GuardName union and GUARD_DEFINITIONS. Fixed guard count test.

shots.title: Docs + sprint plan artifact
shots.notes: Sprint plan, backlog README row, CLAUDE.md flows section. Trivial.

nutrition.description: Clean build + typecheck + all 1124 tests pass (815 core + 36 store + 199 CLI + 74 MCP) after final commit
5 separate commits for 5 tickets — proper commit discipline. Pushed after completion.
Zero existing test regressions. 41 new tests added (24 core + 8 CLI + 9 MCP). Only the expected guard count test needed updating.

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Very smooth sprint. The plan was well-structured with clear ticket boundaries and the execution order worked perfectly. Each ticket built cleanly on the previous one. The established patterns (nested CLI subcommands, MCP module dispatch, auto-section map generation) made the implementation predictable.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: The flows system is foundation-only — no auto-discovery or `flows update` command yet. When building S27 auto-discovery, the FlowDefinition type already has last_verified_sha/last_verified_at fields ready for a bump command. The handleFlowsQuery function in mcp-tools reads config via direct JSON parse (not loadConfig) to avoid ESM import issues.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: Nothing major — this was a textbook feature sprint. The only two hiccups were both predictable: ESM require() in MCP tools (known gotcha) and the guard count test update (always happens when adding a guard).
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: Auto-discovery from static code analysis (S27) would make flows self-maintaining. Also, the stale-flows guard is registered but not yet implemented as a handler — wiring it up would give real-time staleness warnings during coding.

bunker_locations: ESM modules cannot use require() — use direct fs reads or top-level imports for config resolution in MCP tools
Adding a guard to GUARD_DEFINITIONS requires updating the guard count test in packages/core/tests/guard.test.ts
yardage_book_updates: packages/core/src/flows.ts: FlowStep, FlowDefinition, FlowsFile types + parseFlows, validateFlows, checkFlowStaleness, loadFlows
packages/core/src/config.ts: Added flowsPath field to SlopeConfig with default .slope/flows.json
packages/core/src/guard.ts: Added stale-flows guard to GuardName union and GUARD_DEFINITIONS
packages/cli/src/commands/flows.ts: init/list/check subcommands for flow management
packages/cli/src/commands/map.ts: generateFlowsSummary + flows auto-section in CODEBASE.md template
packages/mcp-tools/src/index.ts: flows module in search Zod enum + handleFlowsQuery handler
packages/mcp-tools/src/registry.ts: Flow function entries + FlowStep/FlowDefinition/FlowsFile type definitions
course_management_notes: 5 shots: 3 in_the_hole + 2 green — 2 minor hazards (ESM require, guard count test), zero penalties, bogey (+1)
16 files changed across 5 commits, 1215 lines added, 9 removed
New subsystem across 3 packages (core, cli, mcp-tools) following established patterns
Total test count: 1071 → 1124 (+41 new flow tracking tests + 12 from S25 hazard severity)
